response.justification: The candidate has a broad and impressive polyglot background spanning 15+ years, with genuine high-scale distributed systems exposure (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic) and consistent staff-level seniority. However, the role's hard minimum of 8 years C++ experience is a near-total disqualifier — the resume reflects approximately 6 months of professional C/C++ work at National Instruments with no mention of C++ in the skills section. Beyond the C++ gap, the candidate's stack is fundamentally web/TypeScript/Node-oriented, with no cloud compute platform, IaaS, or hardware-adjacent infrastructure experience. Mentoring and community contributions are positive signals, but there is no evidence of formal technical leadership over engineering teams in a cloud or systems context.
strengths: Demonstrated high-scale distributed systems work (micro-services at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency DLang tooling)
Consistent staff-level seniority with community knowledge-sharing: organized Refactoring Dojos, delivered conference talks, and built open-source testing tooling
Polyglot and infrastructure-aware: Kubernetes, AWS CDK, Docker, CI/CD pipelines, and event-driven platform architecture show systems breadth
concerns: C++ experience is approximately 6 months (NI LabWindows, Dec 2014–Jun 2015) against an 8-year hard minimum — the most critical gap in the entire profile
No cloud compute platform, bare-metal, or IaaS-level infrastructure work; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz is application-layer, not platform engineering
No formal technical lead role overseeing engineers on a systems or cloud team; staff titles reflect IC seniority, not the cross-team leadership mandate this role requires
